parameters: Beast:
Hissssss!
tachie4
I'm sorry, that beast doesn't like people much. Even if I pick it up,
it desperately tries to wiggle away.
But when I'm working? He always comes along and plonks himself
right in front of my monitor. He's such a little bastard.
Beast:
Hissssss!
tachie4
Tachie showName Akai Mato
Did you want to ask me something?
Who are you?
Achievements please
Autograph please
Did you make this whole game by yourself?
Did King Exit make a profit?
Did Shade really make your BGM?
Who are you?
Me? Allow me to reintroduce myself!
I'm Akai Mato, the creator of Demons Roots! This is my third RPG.
I created two others before this one, King's Exit and Tenka Ran RPG.
I actually used to be an 18+ manga artist, but after finding out how
much fun games are to make, I decided to switch careers!
sns1
P_CALL_CE 2 379 1 ON
sns2
P_CALL_CE 3 380 1 ON
You can also find me on social media. I have both a Twitter account
and a YouTube account.
Click on the icon and it'll take you straight there. Feel free to
check it out.
sns5
P_CALL_CE 4 375 1 ON
I also post my latest game updates on this supporter site here.
Click on the icon and it'll take you straight there.
If you're interested in my future projects, this is the perfect place
to keep up to date! I'll be waiting for you!
Achievements please
Aw... You're just straight-up asking me? It's way more fun when
one pops up after you've worked hard to accomplish something.
Well, I guess finding this place could be considered achievement-
worthy. Okay, here you go.
実績 132
How many achievements do you have so far? I put in quite a few,
so it may be difficult to unlock them all.
They're really for those who like to play a game to completion,
so take it at your own pace.
Huh? You don't have any to hand in... Ah! You used Cinematic
Mode, didn't you!?
Yeah, achievements are really for gamers who live to game.
You're not that interested in them. I get it.
Well, if you ever change your mind, please have fun enjoying the
game in Normal Mode!☆
Autograph please
Jeez, you're so greedy! Alright then, take it!
sign2
I get it. You really wanted an autograph from THE Akai Mato, right?
Because you respect me so much.
My sins are many and terrible... I made a game so good that loads
of people enjoyed it. What an awful thing to do.
Haha! I say that, but it'd be really bad if it crashed and burned and
you demanded your money back! ...Please no, please no, please no,
please no, please no!
Did you make this whole game by yourself?
Yep, I made it all by myself. It took me four whole years to finish.
Still, I got a lot of support from ci-en, so it didn't feel like I was alone.
I had people pushing me to live up to expectations.
I even had original BGM and pixel art made up for it! Ahhh, I'm so
grateful for everything!
I'd really like to keep on making games with the help of all
my supporters! Though that dream will never come true if
Demons Roots flops! Ahaha!
Did King Exit make a profit?
Wow, we've been talking about money a lot. This game is about
dreams. Let's change the subject. Oh, but I'll tell you one thing.
When I started out, my games didn't sell very quickly and I thought
I was gonna die, but in the end I was able to create Demons Roots!
If you look at the number of sales it might seem like a profit,
but spread out over three years of work... I wonder what the
hourly wage would end up being.
But that's okay! King Exit is well loved! Even now, I still get people
sending me messages saying they enjoyed it!
I'll be honest, the more I peak as a creator, the more compliments
I receive. It really fills me with emotion.
I hope Demons Roots will be received the same way, but I won't
know until it goes on sale. What do you think? Did you enjoy it,
even a little bit?
Did Shade really make your BGM?
Did you notice how cool the battle music was?
I asked THE Shade to compose for me! And he agreed!
Incredible! THE Shade!
I actually spoke to him on the phone! Amazing, right!? I had a
conversation with THE Shade! Kyaaah!
Oh, and I got permission for something! Here, the speaker!
Take a listen!
I was only able to summon the courage and funds to commission
this music thanks to all of my supporters! Thank you, all of you!
Thank you so much!
black
Tachie showName ？？？
Huh? No way... Someone's inside the house!?
Did I leave the door unlocked!? Even so, I can't believe they just
let themselves into someone else's house! Scary!
A thief!? A robber!? An intruder!? Ahhh! Phone! What was the
number!? 911!?
tachie4
Tachie showName Akai Mato
Ohhh! It's just you, \v[128]! Phew! Thanks for all your hard work!
I'm the creator, Akai Mato!
Oh, it's just the player! Phew! Thanks for all your hard work!
I'm the creator, Akai Mato!
Oh, of course! This is an RPG, so it's normal to let yourself into
other people's houses! I've done it too, don't worry!
This is the room where I do my work. I'm here 24/7, so come back
anytime. Sorry it's so small.
Even so... Hmm...
tachie4
Tachie showName Akai Mato
Ohoh... Ohohoh... No, it's nothing. Ohohoboobs...
No, no! I put so much effort into these character designs!
Stripping them naked is just...! Ohohohohoh...
You're so honest about your desires! Here, take this autograph!
Tachie showName Akai Mato
What? Why not naked? And after I went to all that effort to set
it up too...
Wow, you're playing on a high difficulty? You're good. I'd never be
able to manage it myself.
Here, you can have this autograph for being such a great gamer.
sign1
*Clap* *Clap* *Clap*! It's a medal of honor for all your efforts!
You should wear it proudly!
Oh, by the way! The standing image is of my alter ego
and mascot, Mato-chan!
Since Mato means "target," Mato-chan is always being stabbed by
arrows. It has a kind of tragic appeal, right? Maybe I should whip
up some merch.
Alright, I need to get back to work... Ah, but you came all this way.
Let's chat some more.
Ask me anything!
A
<AreaEvent:2x1>
D
tachie4
Tachie showName Akai Mato
This speaker's actually specially made.
The Demon Roots battle music was handled by the THE Shade,
you know.
You can also use the Sound Test to listen to the seven tracks he
composed, but this speaker's special!
You can hear the "roughs" of the music! Woo-hoo! *Clap* *Clap* Clap*!
A rough in music is like a rough sketch in art.
This is the only place in the world you can hear it! How lucky!
I bet you're glad you came all the way down here now, right!?
D
Tachie showName Akai Mato
Alright, what do you want to hear?
Who's Shade?
Rough: Revenge the Demons
Rough: Change the Beats
Rough: BLOSSOM
Rough: ENDLESS ENGRAVE
Rough: regurgitation
Who's Shade?
I'll do a short intro for those who aren't in the know. Shade hasn't
just composed tracks for AliceSoft's Sengoku Rance and Mamatoto...
Starting with Az*r L*ne, he started making songs for modern games
and anime! He's an incredible composer!
Thank you so much, Shade, for accepting a commission from a
nobody like me! And the other circles! You're a god!
sns3
P_CALL_CE 2 381 1 ON
sns4
P_CALL_CE 3 382 1 ON
Shade's social media is over here! Click on the icon to open the links in
your browser! The BGM is available on YouTube, so please check it out!
Rough: Revenge the Demons
Tachie showName Revenge_the_Demons
This is the first rough I received. Crazy, right?
I wanted a song evokes an image of Deathpolca playing the piano,
so Shade composed it in a way that the piano and guitar are
competing with each other, leading to this final product.
Since Shade said he'd leave the name up to me, I came up
with one myself.
The demons, defeated by the humans, are fighting to make a
comeback... Normal battle music.
And "demon" is also a word that's used to smear someone.
Even the abused slaves are called demons...
So I wanted a name that included both demonkind's revenge and the
recovery of the slaves. In the end, I chose "Revenge the Demons."
Rough: Change the Beats
Tachie showName Change_the_Beats
This one really picks up in the latter half. When I first heard it,
I got goosebumps all over.
In the middle, it suddenly bursts open. If you're a fan of Shade,
it'll probably bring some of his other works to mind. I'm so
touched to be able to display it in my own commissioned work.
I wanted to include the word "change" in the title since this song is
used as battle music in the second half of the game.
Especially since it becomes a regular battle theme after the demons
overcome the berserk incident and the path splits from King Exit.
With these beats of change, I decided to go with "Change the Beats"
as the name.
Rough: BLOSSOM
Tachie showName BLOSSAM
As a player, this song may be the one that leaves the most striking
impression. It's the Sage battle BGM from Deathpolca's awakening.
When I commissioned it, all I had of the awakened Deathpolca was
her standing image. It's incredible that Shade was able to put this
rough together based off a single image.
I received the finished song, but after completing the scene,
I decided I wanted more spice to it and asked for a different
version. That's the one we have here.
As for the name, to blossom means to bloom or to flower.
BLOSSOM, to me, evokes a strong image of flowers blooming on a
fruit tree. Like the flowers on a certain someone's head.
It especially makes me think of the scene where the Black Onion
becomes strong and beautiful. So after putting the two together,
BLOSSOM was the only name that came to mind.
Rough: ENDLESS ENGRAVE
Tachie showName ENDLESS_ENGRAVE
The final battle of Demons Roots... I was planning on putting my all
into it, so it had to have the best possible BGM.
I was really particular with how I wanted it, so it required a lot of
work. Shade put a lot of his soul into it.
I was so moved when I received the rough. He put so much work
into a track for a nameless person like me... Only a real scumbag
wouldn't be moved by that kind of effort.
I always intended to do my best with this game, but after I got
Shade's incredible music, I really wanted it to be worthy of his
work. Still, it's not easy to make dreams a reality.
That's why I wanted to take advantage of this unique opportunity!
I wanted to use all my ability to make the game, characters,
and scenario lift this song up to the heavens!
*Pant* *Pant*... I got a bit carried away there. Sorry if I seem like
I'm getting ahead of myself. I know that as a creator, I need to
maintain a sense of humility or I'll be shooting myself in the foot.
This track just lights the fire of passion inside me. I hope you'll
forgive my temporary slip.
With regard to the name... I struggled with it for a long time. Two
years after receiving the finished track, I still hadn't come up with
a good name for it.
It has such destructive power, so the meaning's kind of ambiguous.
I thought it would work better with a more solemn title. This song
is the one that lets the game shine at its pinnacle.
Essentially it's the point where the player's emotions are at
their peak. That's why I didn't want to give it a vague title.
It had to have a decisive, impactful name.
After thinking and thinking and thinking it over, I decided to go
with "a seal that will never end"... ENDLESS ENGRAVE.
It has a lot of meanings. Bless Curse was a being who kept cursing
humanity, even as he tried to bless them.
The sacrifices carved into the beginning of the curse... Even the
protagonists become a part of it. Even in the future King Exit.
It also shows how strong the one who confronts the protagonists
is, but there's more to the name than just the in-game elements.
The BGM, the scenes, the game... I want all of it to be engraved
into the player's heart forever. Not as a curse, but as something
they achieved after overcoming a real challenge.
I want my games to be the kind where you take something away
from them and use what you've learned to enrich your life.
From here on, the song names become as easy to understand as
I could make them, but I still get kind of excited whenever I see
this name! I titled ENDLESS ENGRAVE with this feeling in mind!
Rough: regurgitation
Tachie showName regurgitation
I asked for a BGM like the climax of a movie, and what a climax I got!
For such a profound track, the scene has to equally solemn. The true
final battle of Demons Roots... Did I make it grave enough?
After receiving the rough, I only made one request. Since the final
boss is that girl...
The first girl you played as in this work comes after you as a
threat. Revised history, prophecies, the ending of King Exit...
I wanted a clock's ticking, full of multiple layers of meaning.
The title is "regurgitation," and the meaning is simply that. I think
a simple title suits a track as heavy with meaning as this one.
Rough: Black line Over
Rough: Ground Dasher
Rough: Black line Over
Tachie showName Black_line_Over
The second I heard this rough, I thought, "That's Shade's sound!"
and got really excited. I grew up listening to this sound.
The idea for the song is "evil vs. evil" or "demons vs. villains."
I don't think there's any composer better suited for this kind of
theme than Shade.
I couldn't say anything about it, from rough to finished product.
It's just too perfect.
For the title, these bad guys are going against common courtesy
and clashing with each other. It's not a place people who lead
honest lives should be stepping into. That's the feeling I wanted.
Cross the line and get dragged into a dangerous fight. That's why
I went with the title "Black line Over."
Rough: Ground Dasher
Tachie showName Ground_Dasher
When Shade gave me this rough, he said, "I woke up and it just hit
me." Any normal person who woke up to this running though their
head would end up dead.
I'd already completed the in-game scene and made a gameplay video
of Kalinka fighting the King of Plana when I commissioned this one.
Shade told me, "I'm honored to make a track for such a wonderful
scene!" With that lethal compliment, I died. Receiving the rough
of the track revived me, though.
My only request was that it'd be better for the player if there was
a lead-in to such a cool song. I figured it would leave more of an
impression that way.
So the finished version had an opening inserted into it. I created
the PV to accentuate it, but... It really is the coolest. I want this
song played at my funeral.
As for the name, it goes without saying.
"GRAND DASHER!" (Mitsuya Yuuji's voice)
Except no, it actually has the meaning of "running across the land."
The demons and slaves, covered in blood and filth, running toward
their fateful battle.
That's the kind of refined coolness I was aiming for. The title of
a track that conjures an image of a brightly flickering life as it's
extinguished... That's where I got "Ground Dasher."
<AreaEvent:3x1>
tachie4
Tachie showName Akai Mato
That's a cat tower. I thought the beast would like it.
There's vomit all over it, and in the hardest-to-clean spots too...
Because I bought the biggest one, it's really hard to take apart and
clean properly. Bigger isn't always better.
clear_all_weathers
Beast:
*Snore* *Snore*...
tachie4
I actually have two beasts. The other one's over there, looking like
a stuffed animal.
He's really cute. Hahaha... Sometimes he likes to sit in a high spot
and vomit, like one of the demonic statues from the underworld.
<AreaEvent:2x1>
Tachie showName Akai Mato
King Exit is the work I released before Demons Roots. Essentially it's
a jailbreak RPG that has a deep connection to this one.
sns6
P_CALL_CE 2 377 1 ON
It's what led to Demons Roots being created and adds a lot of depth
to the story. Demons Roots wouldn't exist without King Exit.
The people who liked King Exit are the ones supporting my current
endeavors. I'm really glad I created it.
I poured three years of my life into making it. If you haven't played
it yet, I recommend taking a look. It has a different taste to it after
you've played Demons Roots.
Click the icon to open the site in your browser! It was put together
by volunteers! I'm sure you'll find it worthwhile!
sns7
P_CALL_CE 3 376 1 ON
This is the merchandise page. I've had a few items made that
I wanted for myself.
The tapestry is for game completion. It's kind of like a trophy
you can put on display. The acrylic key chains are really nice
too. They have a sort of collector's feel to them.
There are also King Exit goods for sale! Please take a look!
tachie4
Tachie showName Akai Mato
That box is full of cans of coffee. I always drink coffee when I'm
working, so I like to have a steady supply.
The money from my supporters is what keeps me buying coffee!
Just fifty yen per can! I can drink so much of it! Life is great!
Tachie showName Akai Mato
Oh, are you leaving?
Leave
Not yet
Leave
Yep! Keep up the good work!
tachie4
Thank you for playing this far. Having other people play the game
I worked so hard on makes the long hours I spent on it worth it.
I'm going to continue working on new games! I hope you'll
look forward to them! Oh, here! Have an autograph!
sign3
Take care on your way back! Don't forget to take good care
of your health!
Eat delicious food and get plenty of sleep and you'll be able
to deal with pretty much anything! Like the things you like
proudly and without shame!
See you! Bye-bye! (*'ω'*)ﾉｼ
